title: AZ Rep. Walt Blackman Presents Abolition of Abortion in Arizona Act to 1,200+ Supporters
author: James Silberman
orientation: A January 2021 news report covering Arizona State Rep. Walt Blackman's introduction of HB2650, the Abolition of Abortion in Arizona Act, alongside End Abortion Now at a Phoenix press conference and rally drawing roughly 1,200-1,500 supporters. The piece summarizes the bill's text, its co-sponsors, the equal-protection rationale, and the next legislative steps.
audiences: Arizona constituent considering whether to back HB2650
State legislator weighing co-sponsorship of an abolition bill
Pro-life activist comparing abolitionist strategy with incrementalism
Reformed pastor or End Abortion Now ally tracking the movement

questions.question: What does HB2650 actually do to Arizona law if enacted?
questions.audience: Arizona constituent considering whether to back HB2650
questions.answer: It redefines 'person' to include the unborn at any stage of development and immediately ends abortion in the state by stripping the existing legal exemptions that shielded abortionists and mothers from prosecution under homicide statutes.
questions.quote: HB2650 establishes that a “Person includes a child in the womb at any state of development” and would totally and immediately abolish abortion in the state in defiance of the supreme court.

questions.question: How does the bill instruct Arizona to handle conflicting federal authority?
questions.audience: State legislator weighing co-sponsorship of an abolition bill
questions.answer: It directs the legislature to treat any federal act, court decision, or executive order that would shield abortion as void, and it commands county attorneys to enforce homicide law against abortion regardless of federal opposition.
questions.quote: Treat as void and of no effect any and all federal acts, laws, treaties, decisions, orders, rules or regulations that would deprive an unborn child of the right to life or prohibit the protection of such right.

questions.question: What is the bill's stated philosophical purpose for the unborn?
questions.audience: Pro-life activist comparing abolitionist strategy with incrementalism
questions.answer: The purpose clause asserts equal protection: a living unborn child from fertilization onward must enjoy the same rights and legal protections as any other human person under Arizona law.
questions.quote: Provide unborn children the equal protection of the laws of this state, to establish that a living human unborn child from the moment of fertilization and at every stage of development is entitled to the same rights, powers, privileges, justice and protections as are secured or granted by the laws of this state to any other human person.

questions.question: Who co-authored HB2650, and how much institutional support did it have at filing?
questions.answer: The bill launched with ten co-authors, including members of House leadership such as Majority Leader Ben Toma and Majority Whip Leo Biasiucci, signaling more than fringe sponsorship.
questions.quote: HB2650 debuts with 10 authors, including Blackman, House Majority Leader Ben Toma, House Majority Whip Leo Biasiucci, and Reps. Brenda Barton, Shawnna Bolick, Judy Burges, Frank Carroll, David Cook, John Fillmore, and Jake Hoffman.

questions.question: What specific statutory carve-outs does HB2650 strike from current Arizona code?
questions.audience: State legislator weighing co-sponsorship of an abolition bill
questions.answer: It deletes the affirmative defenses that currently exempt abortionists who obtain consent, providers framing abortion as 'medical treatment,' and the mother herself from prosecution.
questions.quote: The person was the unborn child’s mother.

questions.question: How big was the public turnout, and who spoke at the events?
questions.audience: Reformed pastor or End Abortion Now ally tracking the movement
questions.answer: Between 1,200 and 1,500 people attended the press conference and rally, which featured Pastor Jeff Durbin, Dennis Sarfate, Rusty Thomas, Bradley Pierce, and Blackman himself, among others.
questions.quote: The rally featured speeches from Rep. Blackman, his wife Kristi Blackman, Durbin, Sarfate, Rusty Thomas, Luke Pierson, Zack Morgan, Zach Lautenschlager, and Bradley Pierce.

questions.question: What is the next procedural hurdle the bill must clear?
questions.audience: Arizona constituent considering whether to back HB2650
questions.answer: It needs a committee assignment; Judiciary or Health and Human Services are most likely, with a slim chance of going to Criminal Justice, where Blackman serves as chairman.
questions.quote: The bill now awaits a committee assignment, with the House Judiciary and Health and Human Services committees being the most likely assignments. There is an outside chance of an assignment to the House Criminal Justice Committee of which Blackman is the chairman.

related.title: Abortion Abolition Bill Introduced in Washington
related.reason: Parallel state-level abolition filing covered by the same reporter, useful for comparing legislative strategy across states.

related.reason: Another companion piece on a state abolition act, showing the multi-state pattern HB2650 fits into.

related.reason: Follow-up coverage of Jeff Durbin, who spoke at the HB2650 press conference, and the End Abortion Now coalition behind the rally.

related.reason: Practical guidance for the activist audience that HB2650 needs to mobilize behind Blackman and his co-authors.
